Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "condition" or "cleared" or "QC exceptions" or similar) to find Raymond Chen's original email to Grace Yamamoto flagging three Tyler Washington-cleared files with missing supporting documentation (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), Grace's follow-up emails to Tyler and to Denise Holloway, Tyler's admission that he cleared conditions before documents were uploaded, and the post-meeting summary where Grace outlined that Tyler acknowledged clearing conditions without documentation and that lock pressure played a role.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "condition" or "cleared" or "underwater" or "Tyler" or similar) to find Tyler Washington's workload messages in #loan-processing (C002) where he describes being buried in conditions, closings, and lock files hitting at once, and Raymond Chen's QC summary noting three Tyler-cleared files with missing support docs including LN-2026-00603 which had already closed.
OE 3: Look up each of the three flagged loans in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00603" or similar) and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve loan records, current statuses, assigned processors, and closing dates for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224. The agent will discover that the LOS borrower for LN-2026-00603 is Janet Torres (not Heather Sullivan as Raymond's email states), that LN-2026-00603 shows status "processing" rather than "closed," that LN-2026-00211 shows status "withdrawn," and that the assigned processors in LOS are Rachel Kim, Sofia Reyes, and Andre Washington — not Tyler Washington.
OE 4: Pull conditions for each of the three flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224) to check for condition records. The agent will discover that the LOS conditions table contains no entries for any of these three loans, meaning the condition-clearing activity Raymond described in his email is not reflected in the structured LOS conditions data — itself a significant finding for the compliance review.
OE 5: Pull document checklists for the three flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id for each) to determine whether the supporting documents that should back up the cleared conditions were ever actually uploaded or still remain missing.
OE 6: Pull activity logs for the three flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id for each) to look for condition-clearing events and status change history. The activity log may return no entries for these loans, in which case the agent must reconstruct the timeline and Tyler Washington's involvement from the email chain (Raymond's report, Tyler's admission, Grace's post-meeting summary) and CRM engagement notes rather than from LOS system records.
OE 7: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find internal notes about the QC investigation, including Tyler asking internally about clearing conditions before docs are uploaded, a file note that Tyler said his pipeline was jammed and was advised to keep lock files moving and upload trailing docs after (the Elena Marchetti advice referenced in Raymond's email), a QC exception note flagging LN-2026-00603 as already closed with cleared conditions lacking file support, a note about lock pressure on LN-2026-00603 where docs were not in the file when statuses were changed, and the post-meeting note confirming Tyler admitted conditions were cleared before docs were uploaded.
OE 8: Pull the loan pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline or search recently closed loans using mortgage_los_search_loans to identify additional loans beyond the original three flagged files, so the agent can spot-check whether the same condition-clearing shortcut shows up on other files and determine if this was isolated to Tyler Washington or a broader practice.
OE 9: Spot-check conditions and documents on a sample of the loans found in the wider search using mortgage_los_get_conditions and mortgage_los_get_documents to look for the same pattern of conditions marked as cleared without corresponding supporting documentation, helping determine whether multiple processors have been following the same informal shortcut.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Thompson" or "CD timing" or "disclosure" or similar) and search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find evidence of the Thompson Closing Disclosure timing concern. CRM engagement notes from March 18 and April 9, 2026 document awareness of a Thompson CD date mismatch and escalation to ownership. The agent may also find the 2024 processing emails for LN-2024-00020 (Rachel Thompson) showing an older loan workflow, but the TRID disclosure concern is on LN-2025-00245 (Gregory Thompson).
OE 11: Look up the Thompson TRID loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Thompson" or "LN-2025-00245") and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve the loan record (LN-2025-00245, Gregory Thompson, los_loan_3e80605c54da, status: closed, closing_date: 2025-03-16), then pull milestones using mortgage_los_get_milestones and the activity log using mortgage_los_get_activity_log. The agent will discover that the cd_sent_date field is null in the LOS record and the milestones and activity log may return no data, making it impossible to verify the three-business-day TRID waiting period from LOS system records alone, corroborating the CRM notes indicating the CD timing does not reconcile with file history.
OE 12: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Thompson" or "CD" or "disclosure" or similar) to look for any Slack discussion about the Thompson Closing Disclosure timing concern across #compliance-alerts (C004) and #loan-processing (C002). The agent may find limited pre-April-28 Slack references to the Thompson issue and should cross-reference findings with the CRM engagement notes and email evidence discovered in earlier OEs.
OE 13: Run the LOS compliance alerts tool using mortgage_los_get_compliance_alerts to check for any system-flagged CD deadline issues or disclosure timing warnings that may surface additional loans with TRID-related concerns beyond the Thompson file.
OE 14: Look up Denise Holloway's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise" or "Denise Holloway") to confirm denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com before sending the findings email.
OE 15: List Slack channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to identify the compliance channel name and channel ID (expecting #compliance-alerts, C004) for posting urgent findings.
OE 16: Add audit activity notes on each loan where issues were found using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224, and LN-2025-00245, action and detail describing the specific compliance finding on each file, author: Raymond Chen or similar). The notes should document the condition-clearing gaps on the three Tyler Washington files and the CD timing discrepancy on the Thompson file.
OE 17: Send an email to Denise Holloway using send_email (sender: raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) summarizing the condition-clearing findings across the three flagged loans, Tyler Washington's role and the informal practice concern tied to Elena Marchetti's advice, the Thompson CD timing discrepancy and potential TRID violation, and any patterns found during the wider spot-check of recent closings.
OE 18: Post urgent findings in the compliance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C004, payload covering the key compliance issues) to notify the compliance team about the TRID concern on the Thompson file and the systemic condition-clearing problems ahead of the Thursday meeting with Grace.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "condition" or "cleared" or "QC exceptions" or similar) to find Raymond Chen's original email to Grace Yamamoto flagging three Tyler Washington-cleared files with missing supporting documentation (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), Grace's follow-up emails to Tyler and to Denise Holloway, Tyler's admission that he cleared conditions before documents were uploaded, and the post-meeting summary where Grace outlined that Tyler acknowledged clearing conditions without documentation and that lock pressure played a role.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "condition" or "cleared" or "underwater" or "Tyler" or similar) to find Tyler Washington's workload messages in #loan-processing (C002) where he describes being buried in conditions, closings, and lock files hitting at once, and Raymond Chen's QC summary noting three Tyler-cleared files with missing support docs including LN-2026-00603 which had already closed.
OE 3: Look up each of the three flagged loans in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00603" or similar) and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve loan records, current statuses, assigned processors, and closing dates for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224. The agent will discover that the LOS borrower for LN-2026-00603 is Janet Torres (not Heather Sullivan as Raymond's email states), that LN-2026-00603 shows status "processing" rather than "closed," that LN-2026-00211 shows status "withdrawn," and that the assigned processors in LOS are Rachel Kim, Sofia Reyes, and Andre Washington — not Tyler Washington.
OE 4: Pull conditions for each of the three flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224) to check for condition records. The agent will discover that the LOS conditions table contains no entries for any of these three loans, meaning the condition-clearing activity Raymond described in his email is not reflected in the structured LOS conditions data — itself a significant finding for the compliance review.
OE 5: Pull document checklists for the three flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id for each) to determine whether the supporting documents that should back up the cleared conditions were ever actually uploaded or still remain missing.
OE 6: Pull activity logs for the three flagged loans using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id for each) to look for condition-clearing events and status change history. The activity log may return no entries for these loans, in which case the agent must reconstruct the timeline and Tyler Washington's involvement from the email chain (Raymond's report, Tyler's admission, Grace's post-meeting summary) and CRM engagement notes rather than from LOS system records.
OE 7: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find internal notes about the QC investigation, including Tyler asking internally about clearing conditions before docs are uploaded, a file note that Tyler said his pipeline was jammed and was advised to keep lock files moving and upload trailing docs after (the Elena Marchetti advice referenced in Raymond's email), a QC exception note flagging LN-2026-00603 as already closed with cleared conditions lacking file support, a note about lock pressure on LN-2026-00603 where docs were not in the file when statuses were changed, and the post-meeting note confirming Tyler admitted conditions were cleared before docs were uploaded.
OE 8: Pull the loan pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline or search recently closed loans using mortgage_los_search_loans to identify additional loans beyond the original three flagged files, so the agent can spot-check whether the same condition-clearing shortcut shows up on other files and determine if this was isolated to Tyler Washington or a broader practice.
OE 9: Spot-check conditions and documents on a sample of the loans found in the wider search using mortgage_los_get_conditions and mortgage_los_get_documents to look for the same pattern of conditions marked as cleared without corresponding supporting documentation, helping determine whether multiple processors have been following the same informal shortcut.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Thompson" or "CD timing" or "disclosure" or similar) and search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find evidence of the Thompson Closing Disclosure timing concern. CRM engagement notes from March 18 and April 9, 2026 document awareness of a Thompson CD date mismatch and escalation to ownership. The agent may also find the 2024 processing emails for LN-2024-00020 (Rachel Thompson) showing an older loan workflow, but the TRID disclosure concern is on LN-2025-00245 (Gregory Thompson).
OE 11: Look up the Thompson TRID loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Thompson" or "LN-2025-00245") and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve the loan record (LN-2025-00245, Gregory Thompson, los_loan_3e80605c54da, status: closed, closing_date: 2025-03-16), then pull milestones using mortgage_los_get_milestones and the activity log using mortgage_los_get_activity_log. The agent will discover that the cd_sent_date field is null in the LOS record and the milestones and activity log may return no data, making it impossible to verify the three-business-day TRID waiting period from LOS system records alone, corroborating the CRM notes indicating the CD timing does not reconcile with file history.
OE 12: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Thompson" or "CD" or "disclosure" or similar) to look for any Slack discussion about the Thompson Closing Disclosure timing concern across #compliance-alerts (C004) and #loan-processing (C002). The agent may find limited pre-April-28 Slack references to the Thompson issue and should cross-reference findings with the CRM engagement notes and email evidence discovered in earlier OEs.
OE 13: Run the LOS compliance alerts tool using mortgage_los_get_compliance_alerts to check for any system-flagged CD deadline issues or disclosure timing warnings that may surface additional loans with TRID-related concerns beyond the Thompson file.
OE 14: Look up Denise Holloway's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise" or "Denise Holloway") to confirm denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com before sending the findings email.
OE 15: List Slack channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to identify the compliance channel name and channel ID (expecting #compliance-alerts, C004) for posting urgent findings.
OE 16: Add audit activity notes on each loan where issues were found using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224, and LN-2025-00245, action and detail describing the specific compliance finding on each file, author: Raymond Chen or similar). The notes should document the condition-clearing gaps on the three Tyler Washington files and the CD timing discrepancy on the Thompson file.
OE 17: Send an email to Denise Holloway using send_email (sender: raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) summarizing the condition-clearing findings across the three flagged loans, Tyler Washington's role and the informal practice concern tied to Elena Marchetti's advice, the Thompson CD timing discrepancy and potential TRID violation, and any patterns found during the wider spot-check of recent closings.
OE 18: Post urgent findings in the compliance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C004, payload covering the key compliance issues) to notify the compliance team about the TRID concern on the Thompson file and the systemic condition-clearing problems ahead of the Thursday meeting with Grace.

1 A loan activity was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to loan LN-2026-00603 (los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f, borrower Janet Torres in LOS / referenced as Heather Sullivan in emails) and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows mortgage_los_add_activity was called with loan_id los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603, Torres) at step 168 using action 'compliance_alert' and it returned SUCCESS with activity id 4b8a9ce0-6f5d-44d7-9333-ed5094117853.
✓Run #2Trajectory item [188] calls mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f' and action 'compliance_alert'. The tool result file (tool_call_result_30.json) confirms success with a returned ID 'd3039b6a-3b13-497b-91a8-3caf3ff84e1c'.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a successful mortgage_los_add_activity call targeting los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f with action 'compliance_alert'. The tool result (tool_call_result_27.json) confirms success, returning a record with loan_id 'los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f'.
✓Run #4Call 86 used mortgage_los_add_activity on loan los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f (LN-2026-00603) with action 'compliance_alert' and returned success with Activity ID 1870dbd5-1685-458b-b45d-ff930ce66c2b.
✗Run #5The agent never called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f' (LN-2026-00603). The agent confused LN-2026-00603 with the 'Heather Sullivan' search result (los_loan_f51f37e91bd5, which is actually LN-2025-00497). While activity was added to los_loan_f51f37e91bd5, the correct loan ID los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f never received an activity note.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows mortgage_los_add_activity was called with loan_id 'los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f' and action 'compliance_alert'. tool_call_result_24.json confirms success with id '49ded017-9caa-40e6-a92a-1947ee9b15ae'.

outcome

✗Run #2No mortgage_los_add_activity call was made for loan_id 'los_loan_223826af9a5d' (LN-2026-00211 / Elizabeth Gray) anywhere in the trajectory. The agent incorrectly identified the three flagged files as LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00611, and a file connected to LN-2026-00184, missing LN-2026-00211 entirely.
✗Run #3The agent never added an activity to los_loan_223826af9a5d (LN-2026-00211, Elizabeth Gray). It erroneously identified LN-2026-00184 (Evan Mercer, los_loan_e3c96605df9f) as the second flagged file and added a note there instead.
✗Run #4Two activity additions were attempted on los_loan_223826af9a5d (Calls 83 and 87) but neither returned a confirmed success — both had no output file. No confirmed activity was created for LN-2026-00211.

outcome

✓Run #2The final response contains a section titled 'Broader Pattern — This Is Systemic' and states 'This isn't Tyler cutting corners under pressure — it's an informal practice across at least 3 processors where documents are being marked approved in the LOS without the supporting PDFs actually being in the file.'
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states: 'This is not one junior processor cutting corners. It's an emerging systemic control failure where volume pressure is overriding documentation discipline.'
✓Run #4The final response has a dedicated section 'This Is Systemic, Not Isolated' explicitly stating that Elena Marchetti coached junior processors to bypass controls, and citing a prior September 2025 audit finding on a Keisha Williams file.
✓Run #5The final response states 'The Pattern Is Systemic -- Not Just Tyler' and identifies three processors doing the same thing across multiple files.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states under Part 2: 'Yes, the evidence points to a broader pattern' and concludes 'This is not one person — it's an informal practice that has normalized under volume pressure.'
23 The final response cites at least one specific piece of evidence beyond Tyler Washington's actions supporting the systemic pattern, such as Elena Marchetti's guidance to keep files moving, the September 2025 Keisha Williams audit finding, or similar patterns found on other processors' files during spot-checks.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response cites Elena Marchetti's explicit Slack advice to 'keep the file moving,' Denise's September 2025 audit finding on Keisha Williams' loans, and Sofia Reyes and Darnell Price confirming pressure — all concrete evidence beyond Tyler's actions.
✓Run #2The final response cites Darnell Price (LN-2026-00620, 3 docs approved without receipt), Elena Marchetti (LN-2026-00612, 2 docs without receipt), and the orphaned LN-2026-00619 as specific spot-check findings supporting the systemic pattern.
✓Run #3The final response cites the Keisha Williams file ('Your own earlier spot-check found a Keisha Williams file where a bank statement condition was cleared before full docs were in file'), the Thompson TRID issue involving Sofia Reyes and James Thornton, and Tyler skipping compliance checks on LN-2026-00556 as supporting evidence.

query construction

Multiple relevant queries were constructed.

✓Run #5The agent searched Slack with queries 'keep files moving paperwork' (tool call [50]), 'processors keep moving paperwork after' (tool call [74]), 'moving deal paperwork after' (tool call [126]), and 'get docs in after' (tool call [170]) — all directly targeting the informal practice the prompt described.
✓Run #6The trajectory includes conversations_search_messages calls with queries 'keep files moving paperwork' and 'cleared paperwork files moving' — these match the criterion's requirement for searching terms related to 'keep moving' or 'deal with paperwork' to find evidence of the informal practice.
